mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over any 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise > 2% MoM
failure_condition: Joint positive shocks to RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive Sharpe ratio for 60-day forward returns over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative short-term S&P 500 returns during inflationary periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not predict negative 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually, with a hit rate below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated policy rates combined with high price levels signal restrictive monetary conditions that dampen economic activity and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0% and CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports sustained corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero Rank IC with 60-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between sluggish GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates a breakdown in consumer spending momentum, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1.5% and RSAFS declines MoM
failure_condition: The joint condition of low GDPC1 growth and declining RSAFS fails to predict negative returns, with a positive hit rate exceeding 55% over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing activity and improved supply chain efficiency, which lowers input costs and boosts short-term corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during recovery phases.
